summary:
Epilepsy is officially included among conditions associated with systemic lupus erythematosus according to standardized queries; it manifests clinically through neuroinflammatory pathways linked to central nervous system involvement. This supports the conclusion that yes—systemic lupus erythematousos can cause seizures.

The final summary should be two complete sentences focusing solely on critical clinical facts:

Systemic lupus erythematosus can cause seizures because epilepsy is formally recognized as a member condition within the SLE SMQ. The link arises from underlying neuroinflammatory and autoimmune mechanisms affecting the central nervous system.

Yes, systemic lupus erythematosus can cause seizures due to its official classification including epilepsy in standard diagnostic criteria and well-documented neuropathological connections like neuroinflammation and CNS autoimmunity.
